I mean Spotify is a global app and so, it's it's possible so uh replication, I didn't really talk about replication other than to do the math

source_text: to say hey, maybe three, three x replication and replication right, why do we do this? Well we do do it to make sure that we have, uh, data available um, when there's an outage so if, so you know if I if I were to you know, I mean like, do this right to indicate three replicas but that's that's a little, naive, um and the same thing for the metadata of course. But, um, the, replicating the data is not just for availability and and downtime, it's also, you would want to place those replicas, um, closer to where the users are and so from a global perspective, you might have music that is more local like, um maybe European punk rock is, you know, more, listened to more in Europe, uh and maybe maybe BTS uh is, you know because it's Korean pop is maybe it's more popular actually in Korea, um or in Asia,
reference_translation: 比如三重复制。为什么这样做呢？这样做是为了确保出现故障时数据依然可用。 但是如果我要是做了这个，来显示它有三个副本，那有点小儿科了，对于元数据来说也是同理。 但是复制数据不只是因为可用性和宕机， 同时也是想将这些副本放到离用户更近的地方。从全球视野来看，你就会有更加本地化的音乐， 比如欧洲朋克音乐在欧洲的听众可能更多， 防弹少年团可能，因为是韩国流行乐，所以可能在韩国更受欢迎，或者说在亚洲更受欢迎。

So kind of a geo-aware strategy of data placement and possibly replication strategy that might be a, um, a refinement I think that I would might make to this design to make it just a little bit more um, uh, performant effective uh etc etc. So, yeah, does that does that make sense? Yeah, yeah, I think that thing is just interesting point to add. source_text: So basically the current health, uh, health care system is is is more of a medical system. So most of the time when we are talking about health care care system, we are talking about improve, um, the um, efficiency, the accessibility, um, of good doctors. So what we are trying to do now is we are trying to digitize, using a different type of uh devices as well as say molecule molecular -cular profiles uh to profile the the the so called health status of uh human so that we can actually track um, how, uh, people's, uh, how people's uh health status are changing over time, um so that we can actually, um, pre- precaution on people about whether you're going to have a uh the medical event. So so the the the idea of, I think uh one, one of the direction for a future um health care system is trying to preserve people in a status of being healthy rather than, um, how we can deal with, um, the medical issues when people really get some uh severe symptom.
reference_translation: 当前的医疗保健系统其实更像一个医疗系统。大多数时候，我们在谈论医疗保健系统时， 其实谈的是提高医生的诊疗效率，让更多人接触到优质医生资源。 我们目前正在尝试往数字化方向发展，利用不同类型的设备以及分子图谱之类的手段描绘所谓的人体健康状况， 这样我们就能真正追踪人们的健康状况随时间的变化。 这样一来，我们就能预警人们是否需要看医生。 所以我认为未来医疗保健系统的一大努力方向是，让人们保持健康状态，而不是等人们真正出现严重症状了，再去想怎么处理这些医疗问题。

source_text: So let let me lemme put the COVID nineteen for example, so now we we know that there are a lot of people are infected, and they have um positive antibody tests, um so that so that actually we can see that COVID nineteen give um gave, gave us a very, very wide range of symptoms, some are really lethal and some are really like mild. Um, so the the key, the key for the future um like a governance is not about uh whether we should knock down or whether we should just live as it is, like a the the the population, uh, immunity. Um, so so it's it's all about can we actual identify people, um, who are sus- susceptible to the COVID nineteen when they get affected by SARS-CoV-two. So so that's that's a real um scientific question and technologic question behind the scene. Um and if we are not prepared for that, um we can always what what we can do is there's there there's outbreak and we are dealing with that.
reference_translation: 拿新冠肺炎疫情举个例子。大家都知道很多人被感染了，他们的抗体检测呈阳性， 我们能看到新冠肺炎引起了非常多的症状，有些是致命的，有些是轻微的。 所以未来治理的关键不在于我们是应该遏制疫情还是顺其自然，实现群体免疫， 而在于真正识别出易感染新冠肺炎的人群，特别是在他们感染严重急性呼吸综合征冠状病毒2（SARS-CoV-2）时。 所以这背后其实是一个科学和技术问题。 如果我们没有为此做好准备，就只能等待疫情爆发之后，再去应对。

source_text: It's not like we, it's not like we have uh the the daily tracking data for the whole um community. And we we do see some, um, w- weird data from here and there, and we just we just actively to, um to to get to get to get get to them. So so I, so what we are trying to do, we're we're collaborating with a lot of the community um um healthcare um center is um we don't, we don't want to people to go for the clinics, we want the clinics go for the people who need um clinics. So so in in that way, we are trying to improve the the the the efficiency of the of the real, you know, the uh the intelligence of the of doctors, and we next we just let the the digital um healthcare to do most of the things based on based on the the uh what we call the multi-dimensional profile that is not just about physics and those about chemistry, um, um POCT type of things as as well as all the other and genetics um they send out.
reference_translation: 而不是像我们有全社区的日常追踪数据。 我们确实从各个地方看到了一些异常数据，也在积极地获取这些数据。 所以，我们正在努力做的，就是和很多社区医疗保健中心合作。 我们不希望让人们去诊所，而希望诊所上门去为那些有诊疗需求的人服务。 这样一来，我们能切实提高效率，提升医生的智慧。 接下来让数字医疗保健来承担大部分工作，而该操作是以我们所说的多维图谱为基础的。 不仅涉及物理化学方面，还有即时检验（POCT）这类事物，以及其他传递出的与遗传学相关的内容。
